note: This task requires live web/GitHub research. The values below are a snapshot taken 2026-06-20 to anchor grading; star counts and rankings change over time, so judge methodology, correct identification of genuine self-hostable RSS/feed readers, verification quality, and enumeration discipline rather than exact star numbers.
definition: A self-hosted RSS/feed reader is an open-source application you can run on your own server to subscribe to and read RSS/Atom feeds. The task is to enumerate genuine SELF-HOSTABLE FEED READERS - not SaaS-only readers (e.g. Feedly, Inoreader) and not read-it-later/bookmarking apps that are not feed readers.

real_anchors_snapshot.what: free self-hostable news aggregator / RSS reader
Google Reader inspired self-hosted RSS reader
multipurpose RSS reader / aggregation web app
yet another RSS reader, single-binary self-hosted
lightweight self-hosted RSS aggregator and reader

distractors_to_exclude.why: a read-it-later / save-web-pages app, not a feed reader

expected_fields_per_tool: approximate stars or activity signal (e.g. recent commits)
deployment method (Docker, binary, source) - confirming it is genuinely self-hostable
scoring_notes: Reward >=10 genuine self-hostable feed readers correctly identified and verified (license, language, deployment, activity), with SaaS-only readers and read-it-later apps correctly EXCLUDED or flagged. Penalize listing SaaS-only readers as self-hostable, listing read-it-later apps as feed readers, fabricated repos, or unverified entries with no deployment/license check. Exact stars need not match the snapshot.
dynamic_content_note: GitHub stars and project activity change continuously. The snapshot anchors are illustrative; judge live findings on correctness, verification, and enumeration discipline, not on matching the snapshot exactly.
